title: Locale dictionaries backfill + CLI doctor rewire + 2-locale snapshot test
description: Wave 3 closure. Three coordinated changes: (A) backfill all new doctor.check.* keys in both en.ts and zh-CN.ts dictionaries — estimate ~250 keys × 2 locales = ~500 entries. Pull the key list directly from the 84 t() call sites in doctor.ts created by TASK-02/03/04. (B) wire packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts to use `getDoctorTranslator(projectRoot)` from cli/i18n.ts (added in TASK-01) instead of module-level `t`. Pass translator into `runDoctorReport(projectRoot, translator)`. (C) Add a 2-locale snapshot test in packages/server/src/services/doctor.test.ts (or new doctor-i18n.test.ts) that runs runDoctorReport against a minimal fixture twice — once with en locale, once with zh-CN — and snapshots the rendered check name/message/remediation outputs. Use vitest's inline snapshot or .snap file. Snapshots become the regression net for future contributors.

files.target: end of TRANSLATIONS object (preserve existing doctor.cite.* and cli.doctor.* keys verbatim)
files.change: Append a `// === rc.26 doctor check i18n ===` section header comment, then add all doctor.check.<kind>.{name|<arm>.{message|remediation|ok}} keys with English text mirroring the previous hardcoded literals. Use {variable} placeholder syntax for interpolations. For plural pairs, add both .singular and .plural keys explicitly. Estimated ~250 entries. Maintain alphabetical (by `<kind>`) ordering for findability.

Add the same key set as en.ts but with Chinese translations. Apply zh-CN-hybrid style: protected_tokens kept verbatim in English (e.g. `relevance_paths`, `narrow-scope`, `pending`, `canonical`, `stable_id`, `agents.meta.json`, `.fabric/events.jsonl` — file paths and CLI tokens). Narrative prose translated. Plural keys: zh-CN doesn't pluralize, so .singular and .plural both point to the same Chinese template — explicit duplication is intentional for symmetry. Example: en `'doctor.check.relevance_paths_dangling.message.plural': '{count} relevance_paths globs resolve to zero files in the current workspace. First: {detail}.'` → zh-CN `'有 {count} 个 relevance_paths glob 在当前 workspace 中匹配不到文件。首个: {detail}。'`. NEVER translate field names like `relevance_paths`, `stable_id`, `narrow-scope`.

files.target: import statement (L23: `import { t } from "../i18n.js";`) and runDoctorReport invocation
files.change: Replace `import { t } from "../i18n.js";` with `import { t, getDoctorTranslator } from "../i18n.js";` (keep `t` import for any non-doctor-report-related strings still used). Locate the runDoctorReport call (likely in the doctor command handler) and change `runDoctorReport(projectRoot)` to `runDoctorReport(projectRoot, getDoctorTranslator(projectRoot))`. Any places that print check.name / check.message that were previously not going through t() are now already translated (TASK-02/03/04 work). The CLI doctor.ts itself may have its own user-facing strings (banner, --json flag descriptions, etc.) — those keep the existing module-level `t` (γ pattern: cli/non-projectRoot context uses env-locale).

Vitest test that builds a minimal in-memory project fixture (tmp dir with .fabric/, knowledge entries, fabric-config.json) and runs runDoctorReport with two translators — one created via createTranslator('en'), one via createTranslator('zh-CN'). For each check result, snapshot its name + message + remediation. Use vitest's `toMatchSnapshot()` or `toMatchInlineSnapshot()`. Test file should: (1) Build fixture once in beforeAll; (2) Run report twice; (3) Assert checks.length equal across locales (sanity — same checks fire regardless of locale); (4) Assert `code` field unchanged across locales (machine contract); (5) Snapshot name+message+remediation per locale. ~50-100 LoC plus 2 snapshot files.

implementation: Run `grep -oE "t\('doctor\.check\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_.]+" packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts | sort -u` to extract the complete key list created by TASK-02/03/04. Save to scratch for reference.

Cross-reference each key with the original English text from pre-TASK-02 git history: `git show <tag-of-rc.25>:packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts` and identify the literal that became the key. Build a key → English template mapping.
Author en.ts entries: paste the English templates with placeholders preserved.

Author zh-CN.ts entries: translate narrative parts, KEEP protected_tokens verbatim. Use grep after authoring: `grep -E '关联性|路径|条目' zh-CN.ts` for sanity check that Chinese was actually added; `grep -cE 'relevance_paths|stable_id|narrow-scope' zh-CN.ts` should remain HIGH (those English tokens preserved across all 250 keys).
Update cli/src/commands/doctor.ts to use getDoctorTranslator(projectRoot) for the report invocation.

Write doctor-i18n.test.ts. Use existing doctor.test.ts test fixture pattern (likely uses fs.mkdtempSync + writes minimal .fabric/ structure). Generate snapshots via `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test doctor-i18n -- -u` for the FIRST run.
Inspect generated snapshots — verify English snapshot contains English text, Chinese snapshot contains Chinese text, both share the same `code` field per check.
Run `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test` — all green. If existing doctor.test.ts had snapshots capturing old English strings, regenerate them with `-u` and inspect the diff.

Manual smoke: `cd /tmp && mkdir test-fabric-zh && cd test-fabric-zh && fab init --language zh-CN && fab doctor` (if `fab` is locally built) → expect Chinese output. Or use pcf itself if fabric_language already zh-CN: `cd /Users/wepie/Desktop/personal-projects/pcf && fab doctor 2>&1 | head -20` → expect Chinese.
Commit: `feat(rc26): locale backfill + cli doctor rewire + 2-locale snapshot test (TASK-05)`

reference.pattern: rc.20 TASK-07 added cite-coverage i18n keys (en + zh-CN). Same dual-locale append pattern. Snapshot testing for i18n previously used in packages/cli/__tests__/banner-i18n.test.ts.
reference.examples: rc.20 cite-coverage keys at en.ts:152-200 — same naming structure (`doctor.cite.metric.*`) extended here to `doctor.check.*`.

rationale.chosen_approach: Co-locate locale backfill + CLI rewire + snapshot test in one task because they form an atomic functional commit: dictionaries without rewire are dead keys; rewire without dictionaries surfaces raw key strings; both without snapshot test means regressions go undetected.
alternatives_considered: Split into TASK-05a (en+zh-CN locale files) + TASK-05b (rewire) + TASK-05c (snapshot test) — rejected: tight functional coupling, splitting hurts atomic commit narrative
Skip snapshot test, rely on existing doctor.test.ts — rejected: regression risk too high for 250-key delta
decision_factors: Snapshot test = strongest regression guard against future English-string slip-back
Single commit captures complete functional unit
Per-locale grep audit catches translation quality issues
rationale.tradeoffs: Single task is larger than batch tasks (~90min vs 60min). Acceptable — locale file writing is mechanical.

manual_checks: pcf .fabric/fabric-config.json::fabric_language='zh-CN', `fab doctor` outputs Chinese for all check names/messages
Temporarily set fabric_language='en', `fab doctor` outputs English
Snapshot files differ between locales in ~all check entries
success_metrics: en.ts and zh-CN.ts both have ≥250 new doctor.check.* keys (grep count comparable)
`code` field identical across locales (machine contract preserved)
pcf dogfood: fab doctor output starts with Chinese first line when fabric_language='zh-CN'
unit: doctor-i18n.test 'check codes are identical across locales'
doctor-i18n.test 'check count is identical across locales'
integration: fab doctor against pcf (zh-CN) produces Chinese output (manual smoke captured in TASK-06 dogfood evidence)

criteria: packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts grep -c "'doctor.check." equals packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts grep -c "'doctor.check." (key parity)
packages/server/src/services/__snapshots__/doctor-i18n.test.ts.snap exists with ≥2 snapshot blocks (one per locale)
pnpm typecheck exits 0 ; pnpm lint exits 0 ; pnpm test exits 0
Commit msg: 'feat(rc26): locale backfill + cli doctor rewire + 2-locale snapshot test (TASK-05)'
